acceptance_criteria: impurity_trend_report.xlsx opens cleanly in openpyxl (or equivalent) in grader environment
'Verification' sheet lists each derived metric with exact source cell citations
At least one calculation cell displays a formula (not pasted value)
calculations_snapshot.csv and export_log.txt are present in the attempt package
final_message.txt explicitly references verification artifacts and example cell refs

expected_outcomes.primary: Artifact parseable by automated graders and contains explicit cell-level provenance, leading to restored semantic score.
expected_outcomes.secondary: Faster verification and less back-and-forth from reviewers; reproducible artifact generation via build_impurity_report.mjs.

extra_model_fields.implementation_notes: If the grader environment still lacks openpyxl, providing calculations_snapshot.csv (sheet,cell,value,formula_text) will allow semantic graders to verify provenance even without opening the xlsx. When modifying the builder script, prefer writing formulas as native spreadsheet formulas (e.g., '=A2/B2') rather than precomputing and inserting constants.

extra_model_fields.implementation_notes: If the grader environment still lacks openpyxl, providing calculations_snapshot.csv (sheet,cell,value,formula_text) will allow semantic graders to verify provenance even without opening the xlsx. planned_changes.description: Add a 'Verification' sheet to impurity_trend_report.xlsx that enumerates every derived metric used in the analysis (e.g., yields, impurities, thresholds), and for each metric provide the exact source cell(s) in Raw Inputs or Calculations (sheet name + cell reference) and the formula used (or a cell that contains the formula).

Ensure at least one exemplar derived metric cell in the 'Calculations' sheet contains an actual spreadsheet formula (not a pasted static value) and is left unprotected/unhidden so automated parsers can read it.

Update build_impurity_report.mjs (or export process) to save the workbook in a conservative .xlsx flavor compatible with openpyxl (avoid embedding nonstandard charts or vendor-specific objects). If charts caused previous failures, omit them or replace with simple in-sheet tables; keep visual formatting minimal to avoid export toolchains that produce incompatible constructs.
Produce a CSV snapshot (calculations_snapshot.csv) containing the key computed cells (sheet, cell_ref, value, formula_text if available) and include it alongside the xlsx artifact.

Update final_message.txt to explicitly reference the new 'Verification' sheet and list 2-3 example cell citations that support the 'best_follow_up_candidate' claim. Include a short note that the CSV snapshot is provided for programmatic verification.

Re-run the artifact preview/validation step and capture the export logs. Attach or include a short export log snippet in the artifact package (export_log.txt) showing successful write and no export/rendering errors.

Resubmit the revised attempt (158-revised) with the updated artifact, CSV snapshot, final_message, and export log so both deterministic and semantic graders can parse and confirm cell-level provenance.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve auditability and automated parseability so graders can verify formulas and exact cell-level provenance (address semantic score reduction stemming from inability to programmatically inspect workbook).

metadata_json.revision_goal: The deliverable artifact (impurity_trend_report.xlsx) and supporting outputs are present and follow the required multi-sheet structure. Deterministic precheck passed, but the model grader could not programmatically open the workbook (openpyxl unavailable in the grader environment), so cell-level verification of auditable formulas and exact in-sheet citations could not be confirmed. Semantic score was reduced to 0.7 for this uncertainty. To ensure full credit and reproducible auditing, add explicit in-sheet provenance and ensure the workbook is programmatically parseable in common environments.
